pageDescription: Identify compatible torrents and automate cross-seeding across your instances.

indexersMissing.title: Torznab indexers missing
indexersMissing.description: RSS automation needs at least one enabled Torznab indexer. Seeded Torrent Search can still run OPS/RED matches via Gazelle if enabled.

rssAutomation.title: RSS automation
rssAutomation.description: Hands-free polling of tracker RSS feeds using your rules.

seededSearch.title: Seeded torrent search
seededSearch.description: Deep scan the torrents you already seed to backfill gaps.
seededSearch.gazelleFullDescription: Gazelle checks RED/OPS for every source torrent. Torznab queries selected non-OPS/RED indexers.
seededSearch.gazellePartialDescription: Gazelle checks configured sites for every source torrent. Torznab queries selected indexers.
seededSearch.noGazelleDescription: Without Gazelle, all matching relies on Torznab indexers.

automation.title: RSS Automation
automation.description: Poll tracker RSS feeds on a fixed interval and add matching cross-seeds automatically.
automation.intervalTooltip: Automation processes the full feed from every enabled Torznab indexer on each run. Minimum interval is {{min}} minutes to avoid hammering indexers.
automation.pickInstance: Pick at least one instance to receive cross-seeds.
automation.allEnabledIndexers: All enabled indexers (leave empty for all)
automation.allIndexersEligible: All enabled Torznab indexers are eligible for RSS automation.
automation.selectedIndexersPolled: Only {{count}} selected indexer(s) will be polled.
automation.selectInstancesToLoadCategories: Select target instances to load categories
automation.selectedCategoriesMatched: Only {{count}} selected category/categories will be matched.
automation.selectInstancesToLoadTags: Select target instances to load tags
automation.selectedTagsMatched: Only {{count}} selected tag(s) will be matched.
automation.noRunsRecorded: No RSS automation runs recorded yet.
automation.requiresTorznabIndexer: RSS automation runs require at least one Torznab indexer.

webhook.title: Webhook / autobrr
webhook.description: Filter which local torrents are considered when autobrr calls the /apply webhook endpoint.
webhook.emptyFiltersNote: Empty filters mean all torrents are checked. If you configure both category and tag filters, torrents must match both.

scan.title: Library Scan
scan.description: Walk the torrents you already seed on the selected instance, collapse identical content down to the oldest copy, and query Torznab once per unique release. If Gazelle is enabled, RED/OPS API matching is also attempted.
scan.runSparinglyDescription: This deep scan touches every torrent you seed and can stress trackers despite the built-in cooldowns. Prefer autobrr announces or RSS automation for routine coverage and reserve manual search runs for occasional catch-up passes.
scan.waitTimeDescription: Wait time before scanning the next seeded torrent. Minimum {{min}} seconds.
scan.gazelleOnlyNote: Recommended 10s+; this is per-torrent pacing, and each torrent can trigger multiple Gazelle API calls.
scan.cooldownDescription: Skip seeded torrents that were searched more recently than this window. Minimum {{min}} minutes.
scan.selectInstanceToLoadCategories: Select an instance to load categories
scan.selectInstanceToLoadTags: Select an instance to load tags
scan.categoriesLoadAfterInstance: Categories load after selecting an instance; you can still type a category name.
scan.allCategoriesIncluded: All categories will be included in the scan.
scan.selectedCategoriesScanned: Only {{count}} selected category/categories will be scanned.
scan.tagsLoadAfterInstance: Tags load after selecting an instance; you can still type a tag.
scan.allTagsIncluded: All tags will be included in the scan.
scan.selectedTagsScanned: Only {{count}} selected tag(s) will be scanned.
scan.addInstanceToSearch: Add an instance to search the torrents you already seed.
scan.searchNeedsTorznabUnlessGazelle: Seeded Torrent Search needs Torznab indexers (unless you are only scanning OPS/RED via Gazelle).

rules.title: Cross-Seed Rules
rules.description: Settings that apply to all cross-seed operations.
rules.noActiveInstances: No active instances. Add instances first.
rules.hardlinkDescription: Create hardlinked or reflinked (copy-on-write) file trees for cross-seeds. Each instance can be configured independently. The base directory must be on the same filesystem as the instance's download paths.
rules.reflinkNote: Reflink mode allows safe cross-seeding of torrents with extra/missing files. When extra files are present, qui-Transmission triggers a recheck; if below threshold, the torrent stays paused for manual review.
rules.enableLocalFilesystem: Enable "Local filesystem access" in Instance Settings to use hardlink or reflink modes.
rules.regularDescription: Reuse existing files in place. Fast, no extra disk space.
rules.hardlinkDescription2: Create hardlinked file trees. No extra disk space, strict piece-boundary check.
rules.reflinkDescription: Create reflinked clones. Safer for extra/missing files, mitigates corruption best.
rules.baseDirectoriesDescription: Comma-separated paths. The first directory on the same filesystem as the source will be used.
rules.fallbackToRegular: Fallback to regular mode on error
rules.fallbackDescription: If {{mode}} fails (e.g., different filesystems), fall back to regular mode using existing files.

seasonPack.title: Season packs
seasonPack.description: When autobrr grabs a season pack, qui-Transmission checks whether you already have enough local data to inject it, then lets Transmission download anything still missing.
seasonPack.enableDescription: Allow autobrr to call qui-Transmission for season pack check and apply.
seasonPack.coverageThresholdDescription: Minimum local coverage before qui-Transmission injects a season pack. If Sonarr resolves the show, qui-Transmission uses the larger season total; otherwise it falls back to playable files in the pack. Incomplete packs are added paused, rechecked, then resumed automatically.
seasonPack.matchingDescription: Season-pack webhook matching rules. These settings only affect season-pack checks and applies.
seasonPack.ignoreRepackProperDescription: Treat REPACK and PROPER episodes as compatible with the season pack.
seasonPack.simplifyHdrDescription: Treat HDR10, HDR10+, and HDR+ as HDR for season-pack matching.
seasonPack.simplifyWebDescription: Treat WEB-DL as WEB for season-pack matching.
seasonPack.ignoreYearDescription: Allow matches when one release includes a year and the other does not.
seasonPack.tvdbDescription: Optional. Improves threshold accuracy when the check endpoint is called without torrent data. TVMaze is used automatically as a free fallback.

categoryRouting.title: Category routing
categoryRouting.description: Route season pack injects to a Transmission category based on resolution and source. Set these to your Sonarr download-client categories so Sonarr imports the pack and preserves hardlinks. A rule with a specific source takes priority over an Any-source rule at the same resolution.
categoryRouting.fallbackDescriptionBefore: Optional. Category for season pack injects that don't match any rule above (e.g.
categoryRouting.fallbackDescriptionAfter: ). Leave empty to follow the Category Mode below.

gazelle.title: Gazelle (OPS/RED)
gazelle.description: Enable direct API matching against Orpheus and Redacted. This runs alongside Torznab; OPS/RED Torznab indexers are ignored.
gazelle.enableDescription: Applies to all source torrents. If possible, qui-Transmission also checks RED/OPS via Gazelle.
gazelle.redDescription: Used for redacted.sh lookups. Paste to replace; clear to remove.
gazelle.opsDescription: Used for orpheus.network lookups. Paste to replace; clear to remove.

matching.title: Matching behavior
matching.description: Control how torrents are matched and which file differences are allowed.
matching.sizeToleranceDescription: Maximum size difference for matching. Also sets auto-resume threshold (e.g., 5% = resume at >=95%).
matching.crossSeedEpisodesDescription: Your local season packs can match individual episode torrents on indexers, letting you seed both formats.

safety.title: Safety & validation
safety.description: Control which matches proceed and protect your existing seeded data.
safety.skipRecheckDescription: Skip matches needing rename alignment, extra files, or disc layouts (BDMV/VIDEO_TS). When this is OFF, those matches are force rechecked by default.
safety.pieceBoundaryDisabled: Piece boundary safety check currently disabled
safety.pieceBoundaryDisabledDescription: Allow matches even if extra files share pieces with content. May corrupt existing seeded files if content differs. Consider reflink mode instead.
safety.pieceBoundaryEnabledDescription: Matches are blocked when extra files share pieces with content, protecting your existing seeded files.

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: Control how cross-seeds are categorized in Transmission.
categories.reuseCategoryHelp: Cross-seeds use the exact same category as the matched torrent. In regular mode, AutoTMM mirrors the matched torrent; in hardlink/reflink mode, AutoTMM is disabled and an explicit save path is used.
categories.reuseCategoryDescription: Keep the matched torrent's category unchanged (no .cross suffix).
categories.categoryAffixHelp: Creates isolated categories (e.g., cross-seed/movie or tv.cross). In regular mode, cross-seeds inherit AutoTMM from the matched torrent; in hardlink/reflink mode, AutoTMM is disabled and an explicit save path is used.
categories.categoryAffixDescription: Keeps cross-seeds separate from *arr applications to prevent import loops.
categories.indexerCategoryHelp: Creates a category named after the indexer. AutoTMM is disabled; save path is set to match the original torrent. Useful for tracking which indexer provided each cross-seed.
categories.indexerCategoryDescription: Set category to the indexer name (e.g., TorrentDB, BroadcasTheNet).
categories.customCategoryHelp: All cross-seeds will be placed in a single custom category. AutoTMM is disabled; save path is set to match the original torrent.
categories.customCategoryDescription: Use a fixed category name for all cross-seeds.

tagging.title: Tagging
tagging.description: Configure tags applied to cross-seed torrents based on how they were discovered.
tagging.rssTagsDescription: Tags applied to torrents added via RSS feed automation.
tagging.seededTagsDescription: Tags applied to torrents added via seeded torrent search.
tagging.completionTagsDescription: Tags applied to torrents added via completion-triggered search.
tagging.webhookTagsDescription: Tags applied to torrents added via /apply webhook (e.g., autobrr).
tagging.inheritSourceTagsDescription: Also copy tags from the matched source torrent in Transmission.

postInjection.title: Post-injection behavior
postInjection.description: Control what happens after cross-seed torrents are successfully injected.
postInjection.autoResumeNote: Auto-resume after injection (when off, torrents stay paused for review)
postInjection.seededSearchDescription: Resume seeded search and dialog-injected torrents automatically
postInjection.externalProgramDescription: Optionally run an external program after successfully injecting a cross-seed torrent. Only enabled programs are shown.

seasonPackRuns.title: Recent season pack runs
seasonPackRuns.loadError: Failed to load season pack runs.
seasonPackRuns.noActivity: No season pack webhook activity yet.

cancelDialog.title: Cancel RSS Automation Run?
cancelDialog.description: This will stop the current RSS automation run. Any torrents already added will remain, but processing of new items will stop.

toast.noIndexersConfiguredDescription: {{context}} Add at least one enabled indexer in Settings → Indexers.
toast.noLocalFilesystemAccess: Instance "{{name}}" does not have local filesystem access enabled.
toast.baseDirRequired: Base directory must be set first.
toast.pickInstanceBeforeRunning: Pick at least one instance to receive cross-seeds before running RSS automation.
toast.enableAutomationAfterIndexers: Enable RSS automation only after configuring Torznab indexers.
toast.saveSettingsFirst: Save RSS automation settings to apply the updated target instances before running.
toast.selectInstanceToRun: Select an instance to run against
toast.enableGazelleDescription: Enable Gazelle matching (OPS/RED) before running with Torznab disabled.
toast.seededSearchNeedsTorznabOrGazelle: Seeded Torrent Search needs Torznab or Gazelle
toast.configureTorznabOrGazelle: Configure at least one Torznab indexer, or enable Gazelle matching for OPS/RED torrents.
toast.selectAtLeastOneInstance: Select at least one instance for RSS automation.
toast.customCategoryRequired: Custom category mode requires a category name

blocklist.selectionUnavailable: Unable to block cross-seed torrents for this selection
blocklist.blockFailed: Failed to block {{failed}} of {{total}} cross-seed torrents

filter.unavailable: Filtering is unavailable in this view
filter.singleSelectionOnly: Cross-seed filtering only works with a single selected torrent

search.noIndexers: Configure at least one Torznab indexer to search for cross-seeds
search.completedOnly: Only completed torrents can be cross-seeded
search.selectTracker: Select at least one tracker to run a custom search
search.selectResult: Select at least one result to add
search.rateLimitSearch: Rate limit active: {{message}}. This protects against tracker bans. Some indexers are temporarily unavailable.
search.rateLimitAnalyze: Rate limit encountered during analysis: {{message}}. Some indexers may be temporarily unavailable. This is normal and protects against being banned. Try again in 30-60 minutes.
search.applyPartial: Added {{added}}, {{failed}} failed{{completedPart}} - check results for details

blocklist.title: Cross-Seed Blocklist
blocklist.noInstancesDescription: Add instances to manage blocked cross-seed infohashes.
blocklist.description: Prevent specific infohashes from being injected on a per-instance basis.
blocklist.blockedHashesDescription: Entries are applied only to the selected instance.

toast.invalidInfohash: Infohash must be 40 or 64 hex characters

dirScan.title: Directory Scanner
dirScan.description: Scan local directories for completed downloads and automatically cross-seed them.
dirScan.noLocalAccessWarning: No Transmission instances have local filesystem access enabled. Enable it in instance settings to use the Directory Scanner.
dirScan.scanDirectoriesDescription: Configure directories to scan for cross-seedable content.
dirScan.addDirectoryToStart: Add a directory to start scanning.
dirScan.noTorrentsAddedOrFailed: No torrents were added or failed in this run.
dirScan.recentScanRunsDescription: Last 10 runs retained for this directory.
dirScan.noRunsRecorded: No runs recorded for this directory.
dirScan.tagsDescription: Added on top of the global Dir Scan tags. Suggested: <dirscan>dirscan</dirscan>, <needsReview>needs-review</needsReview>.
dirScan.orphanWarning: In regular mode, torrents point to your original files. This causes the orphan scanner to treat this directory as a scan root. Any files not matched by a torrent will be flagged as orphans and could be deleted if auto-cleanup is enabled.
dirScan.resetFilesDescription: Mark all files as unseen, so the next scan will re-evaluate them. Existing cross-seeds remain; duplicates are skipped automatically.
dirScan.resetFilesConfirmDescription: This marks all files as unseen. The next scan will re-evaluate everything. Existing cross-seeds are safe; duplicates are automatically skipped.
dirScan.resetScanProgressDescription: This deletes tracked dir-scan progress for this directory. The next scan will recheck the directory and retry all items, including ones that were already finished.
dirScan.resetFilesSuccess: Files reset. Next scan will re-evaluate.

toast.scanCanceled: Scan canceled. Next run will recheck the directory and retry unfinished items.

settingsDialog.title: Directory Scanner Settings
settingsDialog.description: Global settings for the directory scanner.
settingsDialog.matchModeHelp: Strict mode matches files by name and size. Flexible mode matches by size only.
settingsDialog.sizeToleranceHelp: Allows small size differences when comparing files (useful for minor repacks). Keep low for best accuracy.
settingsDialog.minPieceRatioHelp: Only used for partial matches. Requires at least this % of the torrent’s data to already be on disk.
settingsDialog.maxSearcheesPerRunHelp: 0 = unlimited. Useful when you want large directories to finish over multiple runs: each run rechecks the directory, skips finished items, and retries unfinished ones.
settingsDialog.maxAgeEnabled: Only process items changed within the last
settingsDialog.maxAgeHelp: Uses video/audio file modified time (mtime). Fresh subtitles or extras do not keep old items in scope.
settingsDialog.maxAgeWebhookHelp: Webhook-triggered scans ignore this cutoff and trust the imported path instead.
settingsDialog.allowPartialHelp: Matches torrents even when not all files are found on disk. Covers season packs, extras, and partial releases.
settingsDialog.downloadMissingFilesHelp: Downloads files not found on disk for partial matches. Needed for season packs in hardlink/reflink mode.
settingsDialog.skipPieceBoundarySafetyCheckHelp: When disabled, qui-Transmission will block partial matches where downloading missing files could overlap pieces that include your already-present content.
settingsDialog.skipPieceBoundarySafetyCheckDescription: Only relevant for partial matches. Disable (recommended) for extra safety.
settingsDialog.startPausedHelp: Adds Dir Scan matches in a paused state (useful if you want to review before seeding).
settingsDialog.defaultCategoryHelp: Category for injected torrents when the scan directory doesn’t override it.
settingsDialog.noLocalInstances: No Transmission instances with local access

directoryDialog.addDescription: Configure a new directory to monitor for cross-seedable content.
directoryDialog.qbitPathPrefixHelp: Optional path mapping for container setups. If qui-Transmission sees files at /data/downloads but Transmission sees them at /downloads, set this to /downloads.
directoryDialog.intervalHelp: Minimum: 60 minutes (1 hour). Default: 1440 minutes (24 hours).
directoryDialog.matchModeSearchDescription: Search indexers using Torznab for each eligible file.
directoryDialog.matchModeLinkDescription: Skip Torznab. Build file tree from local data and inject directly. Best for re-importing known content.
directoryDialog.categoryLoadError: Could not load categories from Transmission. You can still type a custom value.
directoryDialog.categoryOverrideHelp: Optional. When set, overrides the global default category for this directory.
directoryDialog.tagsLoadError: Could not load tags from Transmission. You can still type custom values.
directoryDialog.regularModeTitle: Regular mode is enabled for this instance
directoryDialog.regularModeHelp: Use <hardlink>hardlink</hardlink> or <reflink>reflink</reflink> mode in instance settings to avoid this risk.
directoryDialog.regularModeAcknowledge: I understand the risks of using regular mode with media directories
directoryDialog.allowedDownloadClientsHelp: Only trigger webhook scans when the Sonarr/Radarr download client name matches. Exact names are copied from Sonarr/Radarr; matching is case-insensitive.

deleteDialog.title: Delete Directory?
deleteDialog.description: This will remove the scan directory and its run history. Torrents already added will not be affected.
